name: Input Validation
def: Checking that inputs meet expected format, type, range and constraints before use.
problem: Reject malformed or malicious input at the boundary before it corrupts state or enables attacks.
mechanism: Validate against an allowlist of acceptable values (type, length, range, pattern) at trust boundaries, rejecting or canonicalising the rest.
benefits: Stops a huge class of bugs & attacks (injection, overflow)
limits: Can give false confidence if incomplete
failures: Validating at the wrong layer; TOCTOU gaps
whenNot: Already-validated internal trusted data (avoid redundant checks everywhere)
myths: "Client-side validation is enough" — always validate again server-side
